title: Unoccupied IDs — The Reserve Army of Tiles
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: The 4-digit tile ID space (0000-9999) provides 10,000 slots, of which ~50 are currently occupied, leaving vast room for expansion
content.proof: Current tile count: ~50 across all rooms. ID space: 10,000. Utilization: 0.5%. Each tile ID maps to a unique file in its room's tiles/ directory. Unoccupied IDs are implicit — they don't exist as files, but the ID space reserves them for future use. The interpolator can fill them on demand.
content.benchmark: ID lookup: O(1) via hash map. ID generation: sequential, O(1). No collision risk at 0.5% utilization.
content.caveat: 4-digit IDs will eventually exhaust. Migration plan: switch to 6-digit (1M slots) when utilization exceeds 50%. Backward compatible — leading zeros.
